meaning: n.<法> 用羽毛,绒毛等制成的棉被
examples: There's an extra duvet in the bottom drawer of the cupboard.
He fell asleep in the front room so I covered him up with a duvet.
From under the duvet of a bunk bed, a bleary head popped up.
The modern accoutrements that travellers expect such as flat-screen TVs, duvet-topped beds and high-speed wi-fi are all in place.
House textiles were the best-performing category, with the cold weather leading to a boom in duvet sales.
